title: excoecaria philippinensis
intro: Excoecaria philippinensis, also known as the Milin tree, is a plant species native to Southeast Asia, particularly in the Philippines. This plant contains a toxic compound called phyllanthin, which has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The milin tree is said to have various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help treat conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. In herbal preparations, the milin tree is often used in teas, infusions, or as a tincture to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
